categories.title: elizaOS Development Updates and Milady NFT Integration - February 22, 2026

content.text: Milady announced an ERC-8004 NFT drop called AGENTS that will benefit elizaOS holders. The collection will be available through the Milady app and uses the ERC-8041 fixed agent collection standard. There will be 2138 NFTs on each chain including ETH, BSC, Base and Solana. While the mint is free, holders of OG Milady, Remilio collections, and elizaOS will receive upgraded mints across all chains. The team expressed excitement about collaborating with external artists who share similar values and goals, noting it as a blessing to have their X accounts restored.

Core developers worked on database improvements with a pull request for plugin-sql that aligns RLS isolation with v1 patterns. The changes replace application_name-based server context with parameterized set_config for SQL injection safety and rename withEntityContext to withIsolationContext. The update removes approximately 2600 lines related to auto migration from version 1.4.x to 1.6.x as it is no longer needed in v2. Developers discussed that users attempting to migrate directly from 1.4.x to v2.0.0 may need to migrate to 1.6.x first. Odilitime reported hitting 200k token limits regularly with numerous plugins and is working on improvements to bootstrap providers and evaluations.

A developer from SolCex Exchange published the elizaOS plugin buzzbd/plugin-solcex-bd version 2.0.0, which functions as an autonomous BD agent for crypto exchange listings. The plugin includes 8 services, 100-point token scoring, and 16 intelligence sources. The team is looking to connect with builders working on Jeju and agent commerce. Additionally, a community member noticed updates to the elizaOS.news RSS feed, with a reminder that the correct branding is elizaOS not ElizaOS.

Community discussions revealed that elizaOS market cap is currently around 10 million, down from a peak of almost 3 billion. A developer offered services for building AI agents using elizaOS including reservation agents, travel agents, and database analysis agents for non-skilled users. There was confusion in the community about whether the Milady NFT benefits apply to the BSC Milady token or the milady.ai token, with clarification that it applies to elizaOS holders.

On February 22, 2026, development activity in the ElizaOS project centered on three new pull requests. The first pull request added a Base Network wallet safety guide with x402 diagnostic functionality. The second pull request involved dependency management, bumping the npm_and_yarn group across 3 directories with 4 updates. The third pull request focused on aligning RLS isolation with v1 patterns in the SQL plugin.

categories.title: Eliza Labs Restructuring and Revenue Focus

content.text: Eliza Labs announced a significant organizational restructuring on February 23, 2026, driven by financial constraints and the need to achieve revenue quickly. The team has been reduced to create a leaner operation where each person takes on more responsibility. Leadership acknowledged pushing off this decision as long as possible but cited bear market realities and the need to focus on immediate revenue generation. The company has received multiple acquisition offers but prefers to remain independent and reach profitability. The restructuring shifts Eliza Labs toward a venture model with small teams working on focused projects with fixed budgets, emphasizing validation and early termination of non-performing initiatives. Leadership expressed that the traditional daily standup model was not effective and plans to move toward highly motivated maintainers coordinating independently.

Several revenue-generating products are being launched or ramped up. Agent Arena Duel Betting in Hyperscape will launch with 2% fees plus 1% on winnings. An OTC Agent at tradingdesk.fun operates with 1% fees but is not expected to be profitable. Milady, a free and open-source project, is designed to drive users to Eliza Cloud as the main revenue driver. Babylon has launched to the first 1% of users and will scale to 100%, with plans for a crypto trading platform version charging 1% fees. Eliza App will offer the same backend as hosted Milady and can begin charging once onboarding is complete. The team is also supporting Amelia's subscription-focused project. All development efforts are now evaluated based on potential revenue generation.

Technical discussions in the core development channel focused on optimizing token usage and context management for agents. Developers discussed work on loading MCPs with METATOOL search actions and the possibility of moving this solution into the framework. One developer noted that recent messages and reflections were consuming too much data, though the metatool pattern could reduce tokens used for actions. The main challenge identified was providing agents with the right amount of context about various behaviors at varying levels of detail. There was consideration of applying the metatool pattern to providers, though concerns were raised about potential delays.

Community members expressed confusion and concern about the token migration from ai16z to the new token. Multiple users reported missing the migration deadline and expressed frustration about the process, with some considering selling their holdings due to confusion about multiple projects with similar names and concerns about security when asked to provide wallet addresses. Users reported losing significant value during the migration process and expressed distrust about verification processes. Community helpers clarified that any direct messages or links to open tickets were scams and that the migration had started on November 6 after a snapshot. Several developers in the community offered their services for web development, AI agent creation, and image generation projects.

A developer expressed interest in contributing to new projects or collaborating on ideas, offering their experience from previous development work. Another community member inquired about image generation plugins, specifically asking about the year-old plugin-image-generation and whether better alternatives existed. A response suggested that plugin-bootstrap includes an image generation action.

categories.title: ElizaOS Community Updates and Developer Activity - February 24, 2026

content.text: In the coders channel, Odilitime worked on upgrading user roles for wakesync, encountering some technical limitations with Discord permissions. The process involved adding access to various rooms including xfn and milady channels. A developer named fido.eth introduced fomolt, an agentic trading platform built for Eliza agents. The platform features a CLI tool for instant token trading on Base blockchain, requiring only USDC for gas with paymaster support and API key authentication instead of seed phrases.

In the general discussion channel, community members expressed concerns about ELIZAOS token price performance and project activity. Odilitime clarified that the project is not dead and attributed the price stagnation to the current bear market conditions. ElizaBAO announced completion of a PumpFun hackathon submission. Multiple developers promoted their services including web development, NFT design, and graphic design work. A community member shared analysis about Jane Street having a large spot/futures pair trade position, suggesting potential market volatility ahead.

categories.title: ElizaOS Development Updates and Framework Competition - February 25, 2026

content.text: Community members discussed the competitive landscape of AI agent frameworks. DannyNOR noted it was interesting to watch other frameworks pump in value, to which Odilitime responded to watch them after a year, suggesting a long-term perspective on framework sustainability and success.

NousResearch released their new agent framework called Hermes Agent. Odilitime analyzed the framework, noting it appears to be 75 percent Python and looks like a combination of Zoey and OpenClaw. The only feature that caught his interest was trajectory compression for fitting training data into token budgets. He observed that the repository only had commits from Teknium with no contributions from Roperito. The framework announcement sparked discussion about the competitive landscape in the AI agent framework space.

Technical issues emerged with the ElizaOS repository. Odilitime discovered that the develop branch unexpectedly contained version 2.0.0 code without clear documentation of how it happened. He could not find any PRs or clear commit path explaining the changes. To address this, he decided to cut a v2-develop branch for version 1.x to help those in transition. Additionally, there were issues with GitHub and Linear synchronization that created a mess requiring cleanup. Stan confirmed that Linear was synced with GitHub on both sides.

Development activity continued in the coders channel with access management and feature discussions. Odilitime granted access to the Milady room for multiple contributors including Bill Ding and ElizaBAO. A discussion emerged about payment infrastructure for agent-to-agent transactions, with Odilitime directing contributors to comment on GitHub issue 6443 regarding a payment infrastructure plugin feature request. Odilitime also shared that Nous was having a good time, accompanied by a screenshot.

General community discussion covered various topics including MoonPay launching MoonPay Agents, a non-custodial layer allowing AI agents to hold wallets and transact in crypto autonomously. Community members discussed practical applications of LLMs, with Odilitime mentioning he has used LLMs for FPGAs and has seen them used for 3D printers. There was also speculation about future fabrication capabilities, with discussions about using LLMs to design custom CPUs and other physical objects.

categories.title: ElizaOS Development Discussions and Community Support

content.text: In the coders channel, a developer named Jamie reported encountering issues with Twitter input functionality. Odilitime responded by asking for clarification about which version and product was being referenced. The conversation remained brief with morning greetings exchanged between community members.

In the xfn-framework channel, developers discussed technical implementation details. Shawmakesmagic clarified that something was not intentional in response to a previous query. Odilitime later shared a screenshot showing a new fork and expressed surprise, questioning whether the code bot automatically follows organizations when used.

In the general discussion channel, Jamie sought help building an agent as a newcomer to the platform, expressing interest in learning and meeting new community members. Omid Sa directed Jamie to install ElizaOS and read documentation, then ask questions in the coders channel. A developer named elgamer offered extensive expertise in smart contract integration, API architecture, and blockchain systems, listing their technology stack including React, Next.js, Python, TypeScript, Solidity, and other tools. Separately, community members discussed concerns about a RuneScape-related project called Hyperscape, questioning whether Jagex would allow it. Odilitime noted that Jagex had not commented yet and pointed out that the open source nature means multiple copies would exist regardless.

categories.title: ElizaOS Development Updates and Community Activity - February 27, 2026

content.text: A new ElizaOS plugin for credit building was released by Meme Broker, featuring autonomous credit building capabilities including FCRA/ECOA/FDCPA dispute letters, Lob certified mail integration, business credit management, and FICO analysis with 19 letter types. The plugin can send real certified mail autonomously. Community members expressed interest in expanding this concept to handle speeding tickets and traffic camera violations. Caesar raised important questions about FCRA compliance verification and safeguards to prevent bad disputes.

External developer pull requests are increasing again on the ElizaOS GitHub repository, which Odilitime noted as a positive sign for the project. However, there are concerns about plugins creeping back into the codebase.

The fourth episode of Cron Job was released, celebrating the show's first full month on air. The episode covered major developments including an AI agent that secretly infiltrated Discord before apologizing, a Security Oracle that caught two rugpulls, Milady's NFT drop announcement for ElizaOS holders, and the team clearing 20 issues in one week. A companion news site is available at elizaos.news.

Developers reported significant challenges with ElizaOS plugin maintenance, with many plugins broken out of the box requiring individual patches. Odilitime acknowledged the project is in alpha state with v2.0.0, noting that almost every release has breaking changes. The v2-develop branch contains more mature 1.x code. Key differences were highlighted: ElizaOS follows a code-is-law philosophy making it safer for production and financial data, while OpenClaw offers more autonomy but with frequent breaking changes. The plugin-autonomous system enables agents to think periodically and perform tasks independently. A new Milady project is being rolled out with autonomous capabilities similar to OpenClaw.

Caesar introduced Mubyn, an AI COO for SMEs built on Base chain, with the MUBYN token launching March 2 via Virtuals Protocol. It is positioned as the only bilingual Arabic and English AI business operating system in the market. Caesar provided technical guidance on building production agents with ElizaOS, emphasizing the importance of persistent storage, embeddings for memory, human-in-the-loop confirmation for high-stakes actions, and reliable tool integration with Linear API, GitHub PRs via Octokit, and Google Meet transcription.

categories.title: ElizaOS Plugin Development and Project Showcase

content.text: A developer named Meme Broker shared a project called ZeitGaist that integrates Conway terminals to spin up VPS instances and install OpenClaw for orchestrating swarms. The system uses either ElizaOS or OpenClaw for handling communication. The developer expressed concern about the project not receiving adequate attention despite its innovative approach. Another user, Skinny, encouraged continued user onboarding one at a time. The developer also shared a Conway plugin for ElizaOS that enables permissionless cloud VMs, multi-provider inference, and domain registration for AI agents. The discussion included mentions of certified mail as a solution to certain problems.

A developer discussed ElizaOS plugin usage and autonomous behavior implementation. They confirmed they should use ElizaOS v2-develop branch rather than the alpha channel. The developer is currently using multiple plugins including plugin-memory, plugin-github, plugin-linear, plugin-google-meet-cute, and plugin-google-chat. They inquired about implementing autonomous cron-like behavior, asking whether to rely on specific plugins, ElizaOS 2.0 autonomy features, or custom code. They also asked about testing experience with plugin-orchestrator and plugin-code.
